title: Where can I buy EoEand best places to order the series
display_title: Where can I buy EoEand best places to order the series
wikitext:
[]: Where can I buy EoE(and best places to order the series)?             Quote Post by lostpainting » Sun Jul 20, 2008 12:34 pm I was reading over at IMDB that EoE is currently out of print, and that a copy of the movie being sold at amazon that includes Death and Rebirth is actually a high quality bootleg. So my questions are: -Is it true that EoE is still currently out of print? -Are there places where I can still find it(legit copy) without having to pay a huge amount of money? -Even if you don't exactly know of any sites where I can get it, where might you recommend that I dig around in order to possibly come across a copy? Edit: I might as well ask these questions while I'm here: Do the few extra segments in Death and Rebirth that are said to not be seen anywhere else make it worth a purchase? Lastly, what are some good sites for purchasing the series? Sure I can check for prices, but along with good prices, what online stores are recommended for both reliability and for getting the product to the customer quickly? I wouldn't know as this is my first time purchasing anime. Thanks for any help.

[]:             Quote Post by master_lloyd » Sun Jul 20, 2008 12:43 pm I bought death + rebirth over Ebay. I think it's a bootleg but it's really good, had subs/dubs and stuff so I can't complain.

[]:             Quote Post by eva unit 04 » Sun Jul 20, 2008 1:35 pm Quick question before i begin hunting for you:Where are you from so i know what region dvd you're after .

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sun Jul 20, 2008 1:42 pm You can still get EoE (if you're talking about the Manga Region 1 release) online as well as at Borders bookstore.

[]:             Quote Post by lostpainting » Sun Jul 20, 2008 3:19 pm I'm in region 1(USA). Edit: Also of interest is this thread (http://evageeks.org/forum/viewtopic.php?t=4402) in which Reichu claims that the newer Platinum and Renewal versions of the series contain altered versions of some episodes(both original and director's cut episodes), which is pretty annoying.

[]:             Quote Post by Sharaz Destler » Mon Jul 21, 2008 11:23 am I got the one I referred to in the "Recent Hauls" thread (which contained D+R and EoE) for $10 at a flea market. However, I only vaguely hinted that it was a bootleg copy as I wasn't sure about the policy on this forum. Picture and sound were pretty good, and it had a few special features on it, so all in all it was a good bargain.

[]:             Quote Post by lostpainting » Wed Jul 23, 2008 1:00 pm Quick question: End of Evangelion is meant to be originally presented in widescreen correct(meaning it's framing is not the same as the series)? Also, I've decided to get the platinum thinpak from amazon.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Wed Jul 23, 2008 1:18 pm lostpainting wrote: End of Evangelion is meant to be originally presented in widescreen.

[]:             Quote Post by Action_Bastard » Wed Jul 23, 2008 1:23 pm I would say go to your local Borders, Barns&Noble(sp*), or Suncoast (if there is still one in existence near you)

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Wed Jul 23, 2008 2:08 pm Mr. Tines wrote: lostpainting wrote: End of Evangelion is meant to be originally presented in widescreen.

[]:             Quote Post by cat42 » Wed Jul 23, 2008 2:19 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: I wonder what happened to them releasing the Renewal version of The End of Evangelion. They totally announced they were doing that like 2 years ago!

[]:             Quote Post by lostpainting » Fri Jul 25, 2008 10:32 am Thanks for the tip, Action_Bastard! I found what I believe to be the last copy of EoE at my local Borders, so I'd also suggest that people searching for it call those stores(I also called Barnes and Noble) like I did. I also purchased the platinum thinpak(new and unopened) from a seller on amazon for $54(not including shipping which I think was around $3).

[]:             Quote Post by Action_Bastard » Fri Jul 25, 2008 10:40 am Just doing my duty, fine citizen. AH ha ha ha ha ha ha haaaaaaaaaAA! Bastard Away!!!! Come Lollipop!
